_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The workspace operates as a freight forwarder — an intermediary that books cargo space with ocean carriers on behalf of cargo principals. Under the IMO Net-Zero Framework structure as approved at MEPC 83:

| Role | Direct obligation under GFI Standard | Direct obligation under Pricing Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| Vessel operator / carrier | Yes — GFI compliance and remedial unit acquisition | Yes — contribution to IMO Net-Zero Fund |
| Freight forwarder (workspace) | No direct obligation | No direct obligation |
| Shipper / cargo principal | No direct obligation | No direct obligation |

The workspace's exposure is indirect: cost pass-through from carriers, and downstream client data obligations arising from Scope 3 disclosure frameworks applied to cargo principals.

**Legal Confirmation Required:** Whether the framework, as finally adopted, imposes any documentation, reporting, or data-provision obligations on forwarders — for example, obligations to collect and pass through GFI data or remedial unit cost line items on shipping documents — cannot be confirmed until implementation guidelines are published at MEPC 84 (Spring 2026). The MEPC 83 approval documentation does not address forwarder-level obligations.

*Source: IMO Press Briefing, IMO, 11 April 2025. MARPOL Annex VI structure: https://www.imo.org/en/ourwork/environment/pages/air-pollution.aspx.*

---
